summary.intent: Refactor gossamer from an Electron desktop app into a VS Code extension to reduce install friction and improve context-switching between code editing and Claude session viewing.
summary.outcome: Successfully scaffolded a working VS Code extension with a sessions list and per-session detail tabs, VS Code theme integration, compact message layout, and sticky scroll behavior. Core indexing logic was extracted into a reusable @gossamer/core package.

repo: Gossamer's data flow: Entire.io CLI writes session data to a dedicated git branch → gossamer reads that branch and indexes into SQLite → UI reads from SQLite
Entire.io presence is detected via .entire/settings.json at the repo root
The root dist/serve.js is the Express server entry point that can be spawned as a child process
packages/core prisma generated client lives at packages/core/prisma/generated/client/index.js — one level up from src/, not two
Workspace package name is 'gossamer-vscode' not 'vscode', so -w flag needs that exact name

code.finding: Uses execFileSync('which', ['node']) to get system Node.js path instead of process.execPath (which points to VS Code's bundled Electron node), avoiding native module version mismatches
Must re-send server_ready on webview.onDidChangeVisibility when visible=true, because dragging a tab to a new window causes the webview to reload from scratch
root and outDir must be absolute paths using resolve(__dirname, ...) — relative paths resolve from CWD (repo root when run via workspace -w flag), not the package directory

Mantine ScrollArea renders its viewport asynchronously, so a useEffect with [] dep to attach a scroll listener runs before the ref is populated — check scroll position inline in the renderItems effect instead
VS Code injects vscode-light/vscode-dark/vscode-high-contrast class on document.body — read it to sync Mantine's forceColorScheme
Prisma import path from packages/core/src/ should be ../prisma/generated/client/index.js

workflow: VS Code extension host runs on Electron's Node.js (MODULE_VERSION 140), not system Node.js (141) — native modules like better-sqlite3 must run in a child process using system node, not imported directly
Vite builds ESM output — script tags in VS Code webview HTML must include type='module' or the browser silently ignores them
VS Code injects CSS custom properties (--vscode-editor-background, etc.) into webview body automatically — use these instead of Mantine's light-dark() for structural chrome to respect user themes
VS Code webview content reloads when a tab is moved to a new window — extension must handle re-initialization
SessionDetailPanel can reuse the already-running server port from GossamerPanel — no need to spawn a second server
Vite entryFileNames/assetFileNames config needed for deterministic output filenames so GossamerPanel.ts can reference them by fixed paths

friction: better-sqlite3 NODE_MODULE_VERSION mismatch (141 vs 140) required architectural workaround — spawning server as child process instead of importing Prisma directly
Vite UNRESOLVED_ENTRY error caused by relative root path resolving to wrong directory when invoked via npm workspaces -w flag
Blank webview with no error — script tag missing type='module' silently failed; required opening WebView DevTools to diagnose
'Error loading sessions' on first open because server startup (prisma schema push) takes several seconds and webview fetched immediately
Server not spawning with no Debug Console output — process.execPath was VS Code's Electron node, not system node; no existsSync check on serve.js
Auto-scroll persisted after fix attempt because Mantine ScrollArea viewport ref wasn't populated when the scroll listener effect ran with [] dependency
Tab title showed full session text making tabs very wide — required manual truncation in SessionDetailPanel
open_items: Electron app (app/) is currently broken — intentionally deferred, to be deprecated later
packages/core CLI tool not yet exposed — only module API for now, CLI deferred
SessionDetail still imports components from relative paths that were copied verbatim — consider deduplicating with a shared components package
better-sqlite3 rebuild story for extension packaging/publishing not yet solved (noted as 'known issue before publishing')
Git branch watcher (.git/refs/heads/<branch>) for triggering re-index not yet implemented — currently relies on manual sync
VS Code marketplace review process and publishing not addressed
ActiveSessions repos tab and 'new session' modal were stripped — may need equivalents in the extension
UI redesign of all views deferred — user said 'keep UI as-is, we can reevaluate later'
